texto: Forget, too, the lamb-y, metaphor-male, the groinless, bourgeois Jesus, with his Easter-egg, candy-store-window eyes ogling the cruciform crosspiece of his eyebrows. If you meet such a Christ on the way, kill him. Do you wish to love? Do you wish to love? Leave love. Love nothing. Life is dark; life is dark at the no-place of the shocked heart cut two by the bone-handled, thrice-bladed Word.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Tim Lilburn que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “Forget, too, the lamb-y, metaphor-male, the groinless, bourgeois Jesus, with his Easter-egg, cand…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, jesus, forget, com foco em reflexao-noturna e reflexao.

texto: Dry snow coming down in the hills. Magpies hair-triggered and thuggish in worn trees. A wall has started to fall in you, it will take years to land.
explicacao: Citação de Tim Lilburn sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Dry snow coming down in the hills. Magpies hair-triggered and thuggish in worn trees. A wall has …” articula reflexao, time, coming em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: One afternoon, disgusted, bravo, you fall asleep.
